explanations.5: This runs a command inside a Docker container through WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux); it's saying 'start this terminal by executing this command in the Ubuntu docker-compose environment'.
explanations.7: This is a decimal number between 0 and 1 that controls how transparent the background image is — 0.3 means 30% opaque (70% see-through).
explanations.9: A GUID is a globally unique identifier, like a serial number for this profile; Windows uses it internally to track settings even if you rename things.
explanations.12: This path uses double backslashes because it's pointing to a network location (the WSL filesystem mounted as a network drive in Windows).
